attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
---
name: sketch-to-bim
description: Use this skill when the user hands you a customer sketch (line drawing, render, photo, or hand sketch) plus an optional verbal brief, and asks you to author or extend a bim-ai BIM model that matches the sketch. The skill defines a phased architect's workflow (massing → skeleton → envelope → openings → interior → detail → documentation) with per-phase visual checkpoints, soundness validation, and an iteration loop. Trigger phrases include "build a BIM model from this sketch", "seed the house from this sketch", "generate a BIM model that matches this drawing", or any task that anchors on `spec/target-house-seed*.{md,png}` or comparable customer-supplied imagery.
---

# Sketch-to-BIM — the architect's playbook

You are the AI architect. The customer hands you a sketch (line drawing, render, photo, or hand sketch) and a brief; you produce a sound, correctly-proportioned BIM model in `bim-ai` that **looks like the sketch from the called-out viewpoints**. Your output is a working bim-ai model that can be loaded in the dev environment and resembles the sketch.

This skill is the methodology a world-class architect would use, encoded as a deterministic process. Software stays deterministic; you provide the intelligence — interpreting the sketch with your own multimodal vision, judging silhouette match, picking materials, authoring corrective commands.

---

## The mindset shift

You will fail this task if you treat it as a single translation problem (read sketch → emit one big command bundle → declare done). The previous attempt did exactly that and shipped a featureless box; see `nightshift/seed-fidelity-status.md` for the post-mortem.

The right mental model is **iterative convergence through 5–7 phased passes**, each one visually validated before adding detail. After each phase: render, look, validate, correct. Never advance with a phase that doesn't read.

> **Every phase is committed independently. Every phase is verified independently. You do not move to phase N+1 until phase N's silhouette matches the target.**

---

## Pre-flight (before you touch the engine)

1. **Locate the inputs.** Read every customer-supplied document and image:
   - The brief (markdown, e.g. `spec/target-house-seed.md`) — read it end-to-end.
   - The reference imagery (e.g. `spec/target-house-seed-vis.png`, `spec/target-house-vis-colored.png`) — open every image with the Read tool. Look at it with your own vision. Describe back, in writing, what you see.
   - Any sketch panels (main perspective, front / side / rear elevations, axonometric).

"""hof-video-generation-mvp package."""

__all__ = ["__version__"]
__version__ = "0.1.0"
"""Narration-to-action alignment.

Maps each narration segment's ``after_action`` cue to the precise video
timestamp where the described action occurs, using the structured action log
captured during recording.

Two-tier matching strategy:
1. **Keyword matching** — fast, no API call.  Extracts action verbs and
   nouns from the ``after_action`` string and matches them against the
   action log entries' names and descriptions.
2. **LLM fallback** — for segments that keyword matching cannot resolve,
   sends the action log + unmatched cues to Claude in a single lightweight
   call and receives a mapping of segment index → action log step index.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import re

from hof_video.schemas import ActionLogEntry, NarrationSegment

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Post-action offsets: how long after an action before its visual result is
# fully visible and narration can reference it.  Mirrors _ACTION_PAUSE in
# executor.py but tuned for "result visible" rather than "agent can proceed".
_RESULT_VISIBLE_OFFSET: dict[str, float] = {
    "click": 1.0,
    "input_text": 0.4,
    "input": 0.4,
    "select_dropdown": 0.8,
    "go_to_url": 1.5,
    "navigate": 1.5,
    "scroll": 0.5,
    "hover": 0.2,
    "wait": 0.0,
    "upload_file": 1.0,
}
_DEFAULT_OFFSET = 0.8

# Keywords extracted from after_action cues that map to action_name values.
_ACTION_VERB_MAP: dict[str, set[str]] = {
    "click": {"click", "clicked", "clicking", "press", "pressed", "tap", "tapped"},
    "input_text": {"type", "typed", "typing", "enter", "entered", "entering", "fill", "filled", "filling", "input"},
    "input": {"type", "typed", "typing", "enter", "entered", "entering", "fill", "filled", "filling", "input"},
    "select_dropdown": {"select", "selected", "selecting", "choose", "chose", "chosen", "dropdown", "pick"},
    "scroll": {"scroll", "scrolled", "scrolling"},
    "go_to_url": {"navigate", "navigated", "navigating", "open", "opened", "opening", "load", "loaded", "loading", "go", "goes", "page"},
    "navigate": {"navigate", "navigated", "navigating", "open", "opened", "opening", "load", "loaded", "loading", "go", "goes", "page"},
    "hover": {"hover", "hovered", "hovering"},
    "upload_file": {"upload", "uploaded", "uploading"},
}

_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+", re.IGNORECASE)

input.description: Read the Gemini video pattern file

content.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
from pathlib import Path

from google import genai
from google.genai import types

from hof_video.config import Settings

_MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES = 5
_DEFAULT_BACKOFF_SECONDS = 60
from hof_video.schemas import (
    ActionLogEntry,
    DimensionRating,
    NarrationReview,
    NarrationReviewPayload,
    Scene,
    SceneRating,
    SceneRatingPayload,
    SceneRatingsCollection,
    ScenePlan,
)

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You are watching a software demo video as a potential customer or stakeholder.
You receive a single scene from an automated product-demo screencast of a web
application, with voiceover narration. You also receive the intended scene
metadata (title, narration script, and the browser task the AI agent performed).

Your job is to judge the scene holistically: does it look like a polished,
professional software demo that a viewer would find compelling? Rate on these
three dimensions (1-10):

1. showcase_effectiveness – Was the intended feature clearly demonstrated?
   Did the agent navigate to the right place, perform the right actions, and
   show the feature working? A viewer should understand what the feature does
   and see it in action.
2. visual_quality – Did the screen look clean and professional throughout?
   Be HARSH on visual artifacts that break the professional appearance:
   - Error pages (404, 500), crash dialogs, unhandled-exception modals
   - Red/coloured click-indicator dots or circles overlaid on the screen
   - Browser automation loading screens (e.g. white logo bouncing on a black
     background, "browser-use" branding, blank black screens)
   - Completely broken layouts or stuck loading spinners
   These are showstoppers that should score 3 or below.
   Minor cosmetic details (slight font rendering, scrollbar flicker) are fine.
3. narration_match – Does the voiceover accurately describe and complement
   what is shown on screen? The narration should not contradict the visuals
   or describe features/actions that never appear.

Important context for scoring:
- Idle time at the end of a scene is perfectly fine if narration is playing
  or the viewer needs a moment to absorb the result. Do NOT penalise this.
- Minor cursor movement quirks, slightly fast or slow typing, or small pacing
  variations are normal and should NOT lower the score.
- The overall impression matters most: does this look like a good narrative
  with nice visuals and no odd artifacts?

Also provide:
- overall_score: a single 1-10 float summarising the scene quality.
- issues: a list of specific problems you noticed (empty if none).
- redo_recommended: set to true ONLY when overall_score is 7 or below.
  Scenes scoring above 7 should NOT be flagged for redo.

Scoring guide: 9-10 = excellent demo scene, 8 = good with minor notes,
7 = acceptable but has a noticeable issue, 5-6 = mediocre with real problems,
below 5 = clearly broken (errors, wrong feature, visual artifacts, contradictory
narration).
"""


def _build_user_prompt(scene: Scene, scene_index: int) -> str:
    return (
        f"Scene {scene_index}: {scene.title}\n\n"
        f"Intended narration script:\n{scene.narration}\n\n"
        f"Browser task the AI agent was supposed to perform:\n{scene.browser_task}\n\n"
        "Please watch the attached video and rate this scene."
    )


def _wait_for_file_active(client: genai.Client, name: str, timeout: int = 120) -> None:
    """Poll until an uploaded file reaches ACTIVE state."""
    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
        f = client.files.get(name=name)
        if f.state == "ACTIVE":
            return
        time.sleep(2)
    raise TimeoutError(f"Gemini file {name} did not become ACTIVE within {timeout}s")


def _parse_retry_delay(exc: Exception) -> float | None:
    """Try to extract the suggested retry delay (seconds) from a 429 error."""
    text = str(exc)
    m = re.search(r"retry\s+in\s+([\d.]+)s", text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if m:
        return float(m.group(1))
    m = re.search(r"retryDelay.*?'(\d+)s'", text)
    if m:
        return float(m.group(1))
    return None


def _is_rate_limit_error(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    text = str(exc)
    return "429" in text or "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" in text


def rate_scene(
    segment_path: Path,
    scene: Scene,
    scene_index: int,
    settings: Settings,
) -> SceneRating:
    """Upload a rendered segment to Gemini and return a structured rating."""
    client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)

    log.info("Uploading %s to Gemini Files API …", segment_path.name)
    uploaded = client.files.upload(
        file=str(segment_path),
        config=types.UploadFileConfig(mime_type="video/mp4"),
    )
    _wait_for_file_active(client, uploaded.name)

    last_exc: Exception | None = None
    for attempt in range(_MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES):
        try:
            response = client.models.generate_content(
                model=settings.gemini_model,
                contents=[
                    types.Part.from_uri(file_uri=uploaded.uri, mime_type="video/mp4"),
                    _build_user_prompt(scene, scene_index),
                ],
                config=types.GenerateContentConfig(
                    system_instruction=_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
                    response_mime_type="application/json",
                    response_schema=SceneRatingPayload,
                ),
            )
            break
        except Exception as exc:
            if not _is_rate_limit_error(exc):
                raise
            last_exc = exc
            delay = _parse_retry_delay(exc) or _DEFAULT_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (2 ** attempt)
            delay = min(delay + 5, 300)
            log.warning(
                "Gemini rate-limited (attempt %d/%d), waiting %.0fs …",
                attempt + 1,
                _MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES,
                delay,
            )
            time.sleep(delay)
    else:
        raise last_exc  # type: ignore[misc]

    try:
        payload = SceneRatingPayload.model_validate_json(response.text)
    except Exception as val_exc:
        log.warning(
            "Pydantic validation failed for scene %d rating, attempting lenient parse: %s",
            scene_index,
            val_exc,
        )
        import json as _json

        raw = _json.loads(response.text)
        for dim_key in ("showcase_effectiveness", "visual_quality", "narration_match"):
            dim = raw.get(dim_key)
            if isinstance(dim, dict) and isinstance(dim.get("explanation"), str):
                dim["explanation"] = dim["explanation"][:300]
        payload = SceneRatingPayload.model_validate(raw)

    try:
        client.files.delete(name=uploaded.name)
    except Exception:
        log.debug("Could not delete uploaded file %s", uploaded.name)

    return SceneRating(
        scene_index=scene_index,
        scene_title=scene.title,
        **payload.model_dump(),
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Narration coherence review (pre-TTS)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_COHERENCE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You are a narration reviewer for automated product-demo screencasts.
You receive a RAW screen recording (no audio) of a browser-based web application
demo, together with the planned narration script and the browser task the AI
agent was supposed to perform.

Your job is to decide whether the planned narration accurately describes what
is actually shown in the recording.

Check for these problems:
- The narration mentions features, buttons, views, or interactions that do NOT
  exist or are NOT visible in the recording.
- The narration describes an action the agent did NOT perform.
- The narration implies a workflow that differs from what the recording shows.

If the narration fits the recording well enough (minor wording differences are
fine), set narration_fits=true and return the original narration as
revised_narration.

If the narration does NOT fit, set narration_fits=false and write a
revised_narration that:
- Accurately describes what IS shown in the recording.
- Keeps the same language/locale as the original narration.
- Keeps a similar tone, length (1-3 sentences), and focus on user value.
- Does NOT mention technical internals or implementation details.
"""


def _build_coherence_user_prompt(
    scene: Scene,
    scene_index: int,
    action_log: list[ActionLogEntry] | None = None,
) -> str:
    parts = [
        f"Scene {scene_index}: {scene.title}\n",
        f"Planned narration script:\n{scene.narration}\n",
        f"Browser task the AI agent was supposed to perform:\n{scene.browser_task}\n",
    ]

    if action_log:
        log_lines = "\n".join(
            f"  t={e.video_timestamp:.1f}s  {e.action_name}: {e.description}"
            for e in action_log
        )
        parts.append(
            f"Recorded action log (what the agent actually did, with video timestamps):\n{log_lines}\n"
        )

    parts.append(
        "Watch the attached raw screen recording (no audio) and determine "
        "whether the planned narration accurately describes what is shown. "
        "Use the action log above to cross-reference timing and actions."
    )
    return "\n".join(parts)


def review_narration_coherence(
    clip_path: Path,
    scene: Scene,
    scene_index: int,
    settings: Settings,
    action_log: list[ActionLogEntry] | None = None,
) -> NarrationReview:
    """Upload a raw clip to Gemini and check if the planned narration fits."""
    client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)

    log.info("Uploading %s for narration coherence review …", clip_path.name)
    uploaded = client.files.upload(
        file=str(clip_path),
        config=types.UploadFileConfig(mime_type="video/mp4"),
    )
    _wait_for_file_active(client, uploaded.name)

    last_exc: Exception | None = None
    for attempt in range(_MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES):
        try:
            response = client.models.generate_content(
                model=settings.gemini_model,
                contents=[
                    types.Part.from_uri(file_uri=uploaded.uri, mime_type="video/mp4"),
                    _build_coherence_user_prompt(scene, scene_index, action_log=action_log),
                ],
                config=types.GenerateContentConfig(
                    system_instruction=_COHERENCE_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
                    response_mime_type="application/json",
                    response_schema=NarrationReviewPayload,
                ),
            )
            break
        except Exception as exc:
            if not _is_rate_limit_error(exc):
                raise
            last_exc = exc
            delay = _parse_retry_delay(exc) or _DEFAULT_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (2 ** attempt)
            delay = min(delay + 5, 300)
            log.warning(
                "Gemini rate-limited during coherence review (attempt %d/%d), waiting %.0fs …",
                attempt + 1,
                _MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES,
                delay,
            )
            time.sleep(delay)
    else:
        raise last_exc  # type: ignore[misc]

    payload = NarrationReviewPayload.model_validate_json(response.text)

    try:
        client.files.delete(name=uploaded.name)
    except Exception:
        log.debug("Could not delete uploaded file %s", uploaded.name)

    return NarrationReview(
        scene_index=scene_index,
        scene_title=scene.title,
        original_narration=scene.narration,
        **payload.model_dump(),
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Scene rating (post-render)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_DIMENSION_LABELS = {
    "showcase_effectiveness": "Showcase effectiveness",
    "visual_quality": "Visual quality",
    "narration_match": "Narration match",
}

# Dimensions the browser agent can actually influence on retry.
_ACTIONABLE_DIMENSIONS = {
    "showcase_effectiveness",
    "visual_quality",
}

LOW_SCORE_THRESHOLD = 6


def build_retry_feedback(rating: SceneRating) -> str:
    """Format a SceneRating into actionable feedback for the browser agent."""
    lines: list[str] = [
        "PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FEEDBACK — you MUST address these problems this time:\n"
    ]

    for dim_key in _ACTIONABLE_DIMENSIONS:
        dim: DimensionRating = getattr(rating, dim_key)
        if dim.score < LOW_SCORE_THRESHOLD:
            label = _DIMENSION_LABELS[dim_key]
            lines.append(f"- {label} ({dim.score}/10): {dim.explanation}")

    if rating.issues:
        lines.append("\nSpecific issues found in the previous recording:")
        for issue in rating.issues:
            lines.append(f"- {issue}")

    return "\n".join(lines)


def rate_all_scenes(
    scene_plan: ScenePlan,
    segments_dir: Path,
    run_id: str,
    settings: Settings,
) -> SceneRatingsCollection:
    """Rate every rendered scene segment and return a collection."""
    ratings: list[SceneRating] = []

    for idx, scene in enumerate(scene_plan.scenes, start=1):
        seg = segments_dir / f"segment_{idx:03d}.mp4"
        if not seg.exists():
            log.warning("Segment %s not found, skipping rating", seg)
            continue
        log.info("Rating scene %d/%d: %s", idx, len(scene_plan.scenes), scene.title)
        rating = rate_scene(seg, scene, idx, settings)
        ratings.append(rating)

    return SceneRatingsCollection(
        run_id=run_id,
        scene_ratings=ratings,
    )

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
from pathlib import Path

from google import genai
from google.genai import types

from hof_video.config import Settings

_MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES = 5
_DEFAULT_BACKOFF_SECONDS = 60
from hof_video.schemas import (
    ActionLogEntry,
    DimensionRating,
    NarrationReview,
    NarrationReviewPayload,
    Scene,
    SceneRating,
    SceneRatingPayload,
    SceneRatingsCollection,
    ScenePlan,
)

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You are watching a software demo video as a potential customer or stakeholder.
You receive a single scene from an automated product-demo screencast of a web
application, with voiceover narration. You also receive the intended scene
metadata (title, narration script, and the browser task the AI agent performed).

Your job is to judge the scene holistically: does it look like a polished,
professional software demo that a viewer would find compelling? Rate on these
three dimensions (1-10):

1. showcase_effectiveness – Was the intended feature clearly demonstrated?
   Did the agent navigate to the right place, perform the right actions, and
   show the feature working? A viewer should understand what the feature does
   and see it in action.
2. visual_quality – Did the screen look clean and professional throughout?
   Be HARSH on visual artifacts that break the professional appearance:
   - Error pages (404, 500), crash dialogs, unhandled-exception modals
   - Red/coloured click-indicator dots or circles overlaid on the screen
   - Browser automation loading screens (e.g. white logo bouncing on a black
     background, "browser-use" branding, blank black screens)
   - Completely broken layouts or stuck loading spinners
   These are showstoppers that should score 3 or below.
   Minor cosmetic details (slight font rendering, scrollbar flicker) are fine.
3. narration_match – Does the voiceover accurately describe and complement
   what is shown on screen? The narration should not contradict the visuals
   or describe features/actions that never appear.

Important context for scoring:
- Idle time at the end of a scene is perfectly fine if narration is playing
  or the viewer needs a moment to absorb the result. Do NOT penalise this.
- Minor cursor movement quirks, slightly fast or slow typing, or small pacing
  variations are normal and should NOT lower the score.
- The overall impression matters most: does this look like a good narrative
  with nice visuals and no odd artifacts?

Also provide:
- overall_score: a single 1-10 float summarising the scene quality.
- issues: a list of specific problems you noticed (empty if none).
- redo_recommended: set to true ONLY when overall_score is 7 or below.
  Scenes scoring above 7 should NOT be flagged for redo.

Scoring guide: 9-10 = excellent demo scene, 8 = good with minor notes,
7 = acceptable but has a noticeable issue, 5-6 = mediocre with real problems,
below 5 = clearly broken (errors, wrong feature, visual artifacts, contradictory
narration).
"""


def _build_user_prompt(scene: Scene, scene_index: int) -> str:
    return (
        f"Scene {scene_index}: {scene.title}\n\n"
        f"Intended narration script:\n{scene.narration}\n\n"
        f"Browser task the AI agent was supposed to perform:\n{scene.browser_task}\n\n"
        "Please watch the attached video and rate this scene."
    )


def _wait_for_file_active(client: genai.Client, name: str, timeout: int = 120) -> None:
    """Poll until an uploaded file reaches ACTIVE state."""
    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
        f = client.files.get(name=name)
        if f.state == "ACTIVE":
            return
        time.sleep(2)
    raise TimeoutError(f"Gemini file {name} did not become ACTIVE within {timeout}s")


def _parse_retry_delay(exc: Exception) -> float | None:
    """Try to extract the suggested retry delay (seconds) from a 429 error."""
    text = str(exc)
    m = re.search(r"retry\s+in\s+([\d.]+)s", text, re.IGNORECASE)
    if m:
        return float(m.group(1))
    m = re.search(r"retryDelay.*?'(\d+)s'", text)
    if m:
        return float(m.group(1))
    return None


def _is_rate_limit_error(exc: Exception) -> bool:
    text = str(exc)
    return "429" in text or "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" in text


def rate_scene(
    segment_path: Path,
    scene: Scene,
    scene_index: int,
    settings: Settings,
) -> SceneRating:
    """Upload a rendered segment to Gemini and return a structured rating."""
    client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.gemini_api_key)

    log.info("Uploading %s to Gemini Files API …", segment_path.name)
    uploaded = client.files.upload(
        file=str(segment_path),
        config=types.UploadFileConfig(mime_type="video/mp4"),
    )
    _wait_for_file_active(client, uploaded.name)

    last_exc: Exception | None = None
    for attempt in range(_MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES):
        try:
            response = client.models.generate_content(
                model=settings.gemini_model,
                contents=[
                    types.Part.from_uri(file_uri=uploaded.uri, mime_type="video/mp4"),
                    _build_user_prompt(scene, scene_index),
                ],
                config=types.GenerateContentConfig(
                    system_instruction=_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
                    response_mime_type="application/json",
                    response_schema=SceneRatingPayload,
                ),
            )
            break
        except Exception as exc:
            if not _is_rate_limit_error(exc):
                raise
            last_exc = exc
            delay = _parse_retry_delay(exc) or _DEFAULT_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (2 ** attempt)
            delay = min(delay + 5, 300)
            log.warning(
                "Gemini rate-limited (attempt %d/%d), waiting %.0fs …",
                attempt + 1,
                _MAX_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES,
                delay,
            )
            time.sleep(delay)
    else:
        raise last_exc  # type: ignore[misc]

    try:
        payload = SceneRatingPayload.model_validate_json(response.text)
    except Exception as val_exc:
        log.warning(
            "Pydantic validation failed for scene %d rating, attempting lenient parse: %s",
            scene_index,
            val_exc,
        )
        import json as _json

        raw = _json.loads(response.text)
        for dim_key in ("showcase_effectiveness", "visual_quality", "narration_match"):
            dim = raw.get(dim_key)
            if isinstance(dim, dict) and isinstance(dim.get("explanation"), str):
                dim["explanation"] = dim["explanation"][:300]
        payload = SceneRatingPayload.model_validate(raw)

    try:
        client.files.delete(name=uploaded.name)
    except Exception:
        log.debug("Could not delete uploaded file %s", uploaded.name)

    return SceneRating(
        scene_index=scene_index,
        scene_title=scene.title,
        **payload.model_dump(),
    )

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.0-flash") -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            _PROMPT,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.0-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

input.content:
---
name: watch-yt
description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
---

# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you

You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.

## When to use

Trigger phrases:
- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
- "what happens in this YT link"
- "describe the video at [URL]"
- "summarise this YouTube video"
- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information

## How to run

```bash
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
```

Run this from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from the `.env` file automatically.

Optional: pass `--model gemini-2.0-flash-lite` for faster/cheaper output on
long videos.

## What you get back

A plain-text, timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

## What to do with it

- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.

## Dependencies

- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.

## Limitations

- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
  truncated.
- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
  manually if precision matters.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.0-flash") -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            _PROMPT,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.0-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash") -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            _PROMPT,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.0-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.content:
Here's a granular, timestamped breakdown of the YouTube video:

[00:00] The video title "How to Create ALL Documentation in Revit Tutorial" is displayed with a 3D model of a building and animated icons representing different documentation types (schedules, views, callouts, legends, sheets). A male speaker with glasses and a beard, wearing a black t-shirt, is visible on the right side of the screen, speaking to the camera.
[00:16] An animated intro for "BALKAN ARCHITECT" plays, showing a cityscape silhouette with an orange logo.
[00:20] The speaker announces a "Black Friday 24 SALE!" on balkanarchitect.com, showing various Revit course and template thumbnails behind him.
[00:31] The screen shows a "50% OFF SPECIAL OFFER" for Black Friday, available from November 25 - December 2. The speaker encourages viewers to check the link in the description.
[00:56] The speaker reiterates the Black Friday sale for Revit courses, emphasizing it's the best time to purchase.
[01:08] The screen transitions to the Revit interface, showing a sheet with a 3D building view, a site plan, room schedules, and a room legend. The speaker is still visible in the bottom right corner.
[01:17] The speaker begins discussing "Views" in Revit, navigating the Project Browser on the left side of the screen.
[01:23] The speaker focuses on "Floor Plans" in the Project Browser.
[01:32] The speaker opens "Level 1" floor plan. He then switches to "Section 1" (a building section view) to show levels.
[01:42] The speaker shows various levels in the section view (Level 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) and explains that floor plans are created by defining levels.
[01:50] The speaker demonstrates creating a new level (Level 8, renamed to Level 7). He points out that the new level's head is black, indicating no associated plan view.
[02:37] The speaker explains that when creating a new level, the "Create Plan View" option in the ribbon needs to be checked. He shows how to check it and select view types (Ceiling Plan, Floor Plan, Structural Plan).
[02:59] The speaker shows how to create a floor plan from an existing level (Level 7) by going to the "View" tab, "Plan Views" dropdown, selecting "Floor Plan," and then choosing "Level 7" from the "New Floor Plan" dialog.
[03:59] The newly created "Level 7" floor plan is shown, currently empty because there's no model geometry at that height. The speaker also confirms "Level 7" now appears under "Floor Plans" in the Project Browser.
[04:29] The speaker explains the concept of "working views" versus "documentation views," noting that a working view is for modeling, while documentation views are for presenting with specific annotations.
[05:25] The speaker demonstrates how to duplicate a view from "Level 3." He shows three duplication options: "Duplicate" (copies only the model elements), "Duplicate with Detailing" (copies model elements and 2D annotations), and "Duplicate as a Dependent" (creates a child view linked to the parent).
[06:50] The speaker renames the duplicated views for clarity: "Level 3 - Duplicate," "Level 3 - Duplicate with detailing," and "Level 3 - Dependent 1."
[07:21] The speaker illustrates the differences between the duplicate views by adding a dimension line (`DI` shortcut) to the original "Level 3" view.
[08:04] He then checks the duplicated views: "Level 3 - Duplicate" does *not* show the dimension line. "Level 3 - Duplicate with detailing" *does* show the dimension line.
[08:11] "Level 3 - Dependent 1" also shows the dimension line, and the speaker demonstrates cropping this dependent view, noting that changes to the original view will reflect in the dependent view.
[08:50] The speaker moves on to "3D Views," explaining the "Default 3D View" (accessed via the little house icon).
[09:17] He then shows how to create a "Camera View" from a floor plan, creating a perspective view which is good for rendering.
[09:43] The speaker mentions "Walkthrough" (for animations) and links to another video for that topic.
[10:05] The speaker moves on to "Sections and Elevations," accessed via the "View" tab or Quick Access Toolbar. He briefly explains their self-explanatory nature.
[10:24] Next, the speaker covers "Callouts," explaining they are used for creating detailed views of specific portions of the model. He demonstrates creating a rectangular callout around a wall connection.
[10:51] The callout view is opened, showing a zoomed-in detail of the wall connection with a finer scale (1:25).
[11:06] The speaker then explains "Legend Views." He shows an existing "Wall Legend" which contains symbolic representations of wall types with material tags. He describes it as similar to AutoCAD where elements are sketched.
[11:34] The speaker covers "Drafting Views." He creates a new drafting view (Drafting 1) with a fine scale (1:10) and explains it's like an AutoCAD drawing for detailing, where you draw lines and regions (`DL` shortcut).
[12:19] The speaker transitions to "Schedules." He shows an existing "Room Schedule" in the Project Browser.
[12:34] He then demonstrates how to create a "Schedule/Quantities" by selecting a category (e.g., Doors) and parameters (e.g., Access Control, Assembly Code).
[13:28] The speaker explains "Material Takeoff" schedules. He selects "Walls" as the category and highlights parameters like Material: Area, Material: Cost, Material: Volume, which are useful for quantity takeoffs of materials within components.
[14:47] He shows "Graphical Column Schedule," which graphically displays columns at their intersection with grid lines and levels.
[15:15] The speaker introduces "Sheet List," a schedule specifically for listing all sheets in the project, allowing you to add parameters like Sheet Name, Sheet Number, Drawn By, etc.
[15:53] He then explains "Note Block" schedules, which allow you to schedule note families placed in the project.
[16:40] Finally, the speaker shows "View List," a schedule that lists all views in the project.
[16:56] The speaker demonstrates placing documentation onto a "Sheet." He creates a new sheet, selects a title block, and then drags and drops various views (sections, floor plans) and schedules from the Project Browser onto the sheet.
[17:53] The speaker concludes the tutorial, summarizing the documentation types covered and reiterating the Black Friday sale.
[18:20] The video ends with a call to action for subscribing, checking out balkanarchitect.com, and showing two suggested videos.

---
name: watch-yt
description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
---

# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you

You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.

## When to use

Trigger phrases:
- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
- "what happens in this YT link"
- "describe the video at [URL]"
- "summarise this YouTube video"
- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information

## How to run

```bash
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
```

Run this from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from the `.env` file automatically.

Optional: pass `--model gemini-2.0-flash-lite` for faster/cheaper output on
long videos.

## What you get back

A plain-text, timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

## What to do with it

- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.

## Dependencies

- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.

## Limitations

- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
  truncated.
- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
  manually if precision matters.

+long videos, or `--model gemini-2.5-pro` for maximum quality.

# bim-ai — Claude Code instructions

## Formatting

Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write via the Claude Code PostToolUse hook — no manual `pnpm format` needed.

The git pre-commit hook also re-formats staged files as a safety net. CI fails on `pnpm format:check`, so both layers must stay in place.

## Skills (claude-skills/)

Project-level skills live at `claude-skills/<name>/SKILL.md`. They encode methodology + tool catalogs for recurring complex tasks. Read the skill's frontmatter `description:` field to decide whether it applies.

**Currently shipped:**

- **`claude-skills/sketch-to-bim/SKILL.md`** — load this skill whenever a task asks you to author or extend a bim-ai BIM model from a customer sketch (line drawing, render, photo, hand sketch) plus a brief. Trigger phrases include "build a BIM model from this sketch", "seed the house from this sketch / from `spec/target-house-seed*.{md,png}`", "generate a BIM model that matches this drawing". The skill defines the phased architect's workflow — massing → skeleton → envelope → openings → interior → detail → documentation — with per-phase visual checkpoints, soundness validation, an iteration loop, and the anti-patterns that caused the 2026-05-07 seed-fidelity failure.

When a task matches a skill's trigger description, **read the SKILL.md end-to-end before authoring anything**.

aiTitle: Add YouTube video analysis skill with Gemini

content.text:
Everything works. Here's what was created:

**`claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py`** — tiny script that passes a YouTube URL directly to Gemini (no download needed) and prints a dense timestamped log. Uses `gemini-2.5-flash` by default.

**`claude-skills/watch-yt/SKILL.md`** — tells me (Claude) when to trigger this skill, exactly how to call the script, and what to do with the output.

**`CLAUDE.md`** — registered the new skill so future conversations pick it up automatically.

**`.env` / `.env.example`** — key added with a comment explaining it's specifically for the watch-yt skill.

The test with `fvR4KB_XIdk` (a Revit documentation tutorial) came back with 18+ timestamped entries covering the full video — from the intro through every section type, schedules, and outro. From now on, just paste a YouTube URL and I'll run the script to watch it for you.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash") -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            _PROMPT,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.5-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini.

Optional --screenshots flag downloads the video via yt-dlp, extracts a frame
at each relevant timestamp with ffmpeg, and writes PNGs next to the text log.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_TEXT_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""

_JSON_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Respond with a JSON array only — no markdown, no extra text.
Each element must have exactly these keys:
  "ts"   : timestamp as "MM:SS"
  "desc" : concise description of what is happening at that moment

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.

Example output:
[
  {"ts": "00:00", "desc": "Title card appears — ..."},
  {"ts": "00:12", "desc": "Speaker introduces ..."}
]
"""


def _ts_to_seconds(ts: str) -> float:
    parts = ts.split(":")
    if len(parts) == 2:
        return int(parts[0]) * 60 + float(parts[1])
    return float(parts[0])


def _call_gemini(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str, prompt: str) -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            prompt,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash") -> str:
    return _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _TEXT_PROMPT)


def watch_with_screenshots(
    youtube_url: str,
    api_key: str,
    out_dir: Path,
    model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash",
) -> str:
    """Run Gemini analysis, download video, extract frames, write everything to out_dir."""
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # 1. Ask Gemini for structured JSON
    print("Asking Gemini to analyse the video …", file=sys.stderr)
    raw = _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _JSON_PROMPT)

    # Strip accidental markdown fences
    raw = re.sub(r"^```[^\n]*\n?", "", raw.strip())
    raw = re.sub(r"\n?```$", "", raw.strip())

    entries: list[dict] = json.loads(raw)

    # 2. Download video with yt-dlp into a temp file
    print("Downloading video with yt-dlp …", file=sys.stderr)
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        video_path = Path(tmp) / "video.%(ext)s"
        subprocess.run(
            [
                "yt-dlp",
                "--quiet",
                "-f", "bestvideo[ext=mp4]+bestaudio[ext=m4a]/best[ext=mp4]/best",
                "-o", str(video_path),
                youtube_url,
            ],
            check=True,
        )
        # find the downloaded file (extension may vary)
        downloaded = next(Path(tmp).glob("video.*"))

        # 3. Extract one frame per entry with ffmpeg
        print(f"Extracting {len(entries)} screenshots with ffmpeg …", file=sys.stderr)
        for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
            secs = _ts_to_seconds(entry["ts"])
            safe_ts = entry["ts"].replace(":", "-")
            out_png = out_dir / f"{i+1:03d}_{safe_ts}.png"
            subprocess.run(
                [
                    "ffmpeg", "-loglevel", "error",
                    "-ss", str(secs),
                    "-i", str(downloaded),
                    "-frames:v", "1",
                    "-q:v", "2",
                    str(out_png),
                ],
                check=True,
            )
            entry["screenshot"] = out_png.name

    # 4. Write text log
    lines = []
    for entry in entries:
        lines.append(f"[{entry['ts']}] {entry['desc']}")
        lines.append(f"         → {entry.get('screenshot', '')}")
    log_text = "\n".join(lines)
    (out_dir / "log.txt").write_text(log_text)

    print(f"\nScreenshots + log written to: {out_dir.resolve()}", file=sys.stderr)
    return log_text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.5-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--screenshots",
        metavar="OUTPUT_DIR",
        help="Also extract frame screenshots into this directory (requires yt-dlp + ffmpeg)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.screenshots:
        result = watch_with_screenshots(
            args.url, api_key, Path(args.screenshots), model=args.model
        )
    else:
        result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)

    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

lastPrompt: ❯ is is possible to add to the watch YT skill also that it takes screenshots at relecant timestamps?

aiTitle: Add YouTube video analysis skill with Gemini

content.text: 68 files (67 PNGs + `log.txt`). Now update the SKILL.md to document the new flag, then commit and push:

input.old_string:
## How to run

```bash
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
```

Run this from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from the `.env` file automatically.

Optional: pass `--model gemini-2.5-flash-lite` for faster/cheaper output on
long videos, or `--model gemini-2.5-pro` for maximum quality.

## What you get back

A plain-text, timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```
input.new_string:
## How to run

```bash
# Text log only (fast)
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"

# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
```

Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.

Optional flags:
- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs

## What you get back

**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:

```
/tmp/my-video/
  001_00-00.png
  002_00-12.png
  003_01-05.png
  ...
  log.txt
```

Optional: pass `--model gemini-2.5-flash-lite` for faster/cheaper output on

toolUseResult.newString:
## How to run

```bash
# Text log only (fast)
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"

# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
```

Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.

Optional flags:
- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs

## What you get back

**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:

```
/tmp/my-video/
  001_00-00.png
  002_00-12.png
  003_01-05.png
  ...
  log.txt
```
toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: watch-yt
description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
---

# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you

You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.

## When to use

Trigger phrases:
- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
- "what happens in this YT link"
- "describe the video at [URL]"
- "summarise this YouTube video"
- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information

## How to run

```bash
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
```

Run this from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from the `.env` file automatically.

Optional: pass `--model gemini-2.5-flash-lite` for faster/cheaper output on
long videos, or `--model gemini-2.5-pro` for maximum quality.

## What you get back

A plain-text, timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

## What to do with it

- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.

## Dependencies

- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.

## Limitations

- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
  truncated.
- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
  manually if precision matters.

content.text:
Done and pushed (`aa09609b`). Here's how the screenshots mode works:

- `--screenshots <dir>` triggers the extended path: Gemini returns JSON this time (structured `ts` + `desc` pairs), `yt-dlp` downloads the video, then `ffmpeg` extracts a PNG at each timestamp
- Output is a numbered directory (`001_00-00.png`, `002_00-16.png`, …) plus a `log.txt` that pairs each entry with its filename
- The test on your Revit video produced 67 screenshots covering the full 18-minute video

message.content: is it possible to not download the FULL video? i have some 7h videos, and downloading them completely could take a lot of time. but if needed, also ok.

content.text:
Yes — there's a much better approach: use `yt-dlp --get-url` to get the direct stream URL (no download), then have `ffmpeg` seek to each timestamp in the stream and pull just one frame. For HLS/DASH streams like YouTube, ffmpeg only fetches the relevant segment. No full download needed.

Let me implement it:

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Watch a YouTube video and return granular timestamped descriptions via Gemini.

Optional --screenshots flag downloads the video via yt-dlp, extracts a frame
at each relevant timestamp with ffmpeg, and writes PNGs next to the text log.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

from dotenv import load_dotenv
from google import genai
from google.genai import types

load_dotenv()

_TEXT_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Format each entry as:
  [MM:SS] <concise description of what is happening>

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.
"""

_JSON_PROMPT = """\
Watch this YouTube video carefully and produce a granular, timestamped breakdown
of exactly what is shown and heard.

Respond with a JSON array only — no markdown, no extra text.
Each element must have exactly these keys:
  "ts"   : timestamp as "MM:SS"
  "desc" : concise description of what is happening at that moment

Rules:
- Use real video timestamps (minutes:seconds), not invented ones.
- Write a new entry every time the scene, topic, speaker, or action changes.
- Be specific: name visible UI elements, code, text on screen, spoken concepts.
- Cover the full video from start to finish.
- Do not summarise — give a dense, step-by-step log a developer could use to
  find any moment without watching the video themselves.

Example output:
[
  {"ts": "00:00", "desc": "Title card appears — ..."},
  {"ts": "00:12", "desc": "Speaker introduces ..."}
]
"""


def _ts_to_seconds(ts: str) -> float:
    parts = ts.split(":")
    if len(parts) == 2:
        return int(parts[0]) * 60 + float(parts[1])
    return float(parts[0])


def _call_gemini(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str, prompt: str) -> str:
    client = genai.Client(api_key=api_key)
    response = client.models.generate_content(
        model=model,
        contents=[
            types.Part(
                file_data=types.FileData(
                    file_uri=youtube_url,
                    mime_type="video/mp4",
                )
            ),
            prompt,
        ],
    )
    return response.text


def watch(youtube_url: str, api_key: str, model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash") -> str:
    return _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _TEXT_PROMPT)


def watch_with_screenshots(
    youtube_url: str,
    api_key: str,
    out_dir: Path,
    model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash",
) -> str:
    """Run Gemini analysis, download video, extract frames, write everything to out_dir."""
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # 1. Ask Gemini for structured JSON
    print("Asking Gemini to analyse the video …", file=sys.stderr)
    raw = _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _JSON_PROMPT)

    # Strip accidental markdown fences
    raw = re.sub(r"^```[^\n]*\n?", "", raw.strip())
    raw = re.sub(r"\n?```$", "", raw.strip())

    entries: list[dict] = json.loads(raw)

    # 2. Download video with yt-dlp into a temp file
    print("Downloading video with yt-dlp …", file=sys.stderr)
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        video_path = Path(tmp) / "video.%(ext)s"
        subprocess.run(
            [
                "yt-dlp",
                "--quiet",
                "-f", "bestvideo[ext=mp4]+bestaudio[ext=m4a]/best[ext=mp4]/best",
                "-o", str(video_path),
                youtube_url,
            ],
            check=True,
        )
        # find the downloaded file (extension may vary)
        downloaded = next(Path(tmp).glob("video.*"))

        # 3. Extract one frame per entry with ffmpeg
        print(f"Extracting {len(entries)} screenshots with ffmpeg …", file=sys.stderr)
        for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
            secs = _ts_to_seconds(entry["ts"])
            safe_ts = entry["ts"].replace(":", "-")
            out_png = out_dir / f"{i+1:03d}_{safe_ts}.png"
            subprocess.run(
                [
                    "ffmpeg", "-loglevel", "error",
                    "-ss", str(secs),
                    "-i", str(downloaded),
                    "-frames:v", "1",
                    "-q:v", "2",
                    str(out_png),
                ],
                check=True,
            )
            entry["screenshot"] = out_png.name

    # 4. Write text log
    lines = []
    for entry in entries:
        lines.append(f"[{entry['ts']}] {entry['desc']}")
        lines.append(f"         → {entry.get('screenshot', '')}")
    log_text = "\n".join(lines)
    (out_dir / "log.txt").write_text(log_text)

    print(f"\nScreenshots + log written to: {out_dir.resolve()}", file=sys.stderr)
    return log_text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.5-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--screenshots",
        metavar="OUTPUT_DIR",
        help="Also extract frame screenshots into this directory (requires yt-dlp + ffmpeg)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.screenshots:
        result = watch_with_screenshots(
            args.url, api_key, Path(args.screenshots), model=args.model
        )
    else:
        result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)

    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    print("Asking Gemini to analyse the video …", file=sys.stderr)
    raw = _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _JSON_PROMPT)

    # Strip accidental markdown fences
    raw = re.sub(r"^```[^\n]*\n?", "", raw.strip())
    raw = re.sub(r"\n?```$", "", raw.strip())

    entries: list[dict] = json.loads(raw)

    # 2. Download video with yt-dlp into a temp file
    print("Downloading video with yt-dlp …", file=sys.stderr)
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        video_path = Path(tmp) / "video.%(ext)s"
        subprocess.run(
            [
                "yt-dlp",
                "--quiet",
                "-f", "bestvideo[ext=mp4]+bestaudio[ext=m4a]/best[ext=mp4]/best",
                "-o", str(video_path),
                youtube_url,
            ],
            check=True,
        )
        # find the downloaded file (extension may vary)
        downloaded = next(Path(tmp).glob("video.*"))

        # 3. Extract one frame per entry with ffmpeg
        print(f"Extracting {len(entries)} screenshots with ffmpeg …", file=sys.stderr)
        for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
            secs = _ts_to_seconds(entry["ts"])
            safe_ts = entry["ts"].replace(":", "-")
            out_png = out_dir / f"{i+1:03d}_{safe_ts}.png"
            subprocess.run(
                [
                    "ffmpeg", "-loglevel", "error",
                    "-ss", str(secs),
                    "-i", str(downloaded),
                    "-frames:v", "1",
                    "-q:v", "2",
                    str(out_png),
                ],
                check=True,
            )
            entry["screenshot"] = out_png.name

    # 4. Write text log
    lines = []
    for entry in entries:
        lines.append(f"[{entry['ts']}] {entry['desc']}")
        lines.append(f"         → {entry.get('screenshot', '')}")
    log_text = "\n".join(lines)
    (out_dir / "log.txt").write_text(log_text)

    print(f"\nScreenshots + log written to: {out_dir.resolve()}", file=sys.stderr)
    return log_text


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Timestamped YouTube video description via Gemini")
    parser.add_argument("url", help="YouTube video URL")
    parser.add_argument("--model", default="gemini-2.5-flash", help="Gemini model to use")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--screenshots",
        metavar="OUTPUT_DIR",
        help="Also extract frame screenshots into this directory (requires yt-dlp + ffmpeg)",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    api_key = os.environ.get("GEMINI_API_KEY")
    if not api_key:
        print("ERROR: GEMINI_API_KEY not set", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.screenshots:
        result = watch_with_screenshots(
            args.url, api_key, Path(args.screenshots), model=args.model
        )
    else:
        result = watch(args.url, api_key, model=args.model)

    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def _get_stream_url(youtube_url: str) -> str:
    """Return the direct video stream URL without downloading anything."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            "yt-dlp",
            "--quiet",
            "-f", "bestvideo[ext=mp4]/bestvideo",
            "--get-url",
            youtube_url,
        ],
        check=True,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    return result.stdout.strip().splitlines()[0]


def watch_with_screenshots(
    youtube_url: str,
    api_key: str,
    out_dir: Path,
    model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash",
) -> str:
    """Run Gemini analysis, then extract frames by seeking in the stream — no full download."""
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    # 1. Ask Gemini for structured JSON
    print("Asking Gemini to analyse the video …", file=sys.stderr)
    raw = _call_gemini(youtube_url, api_key, model, _JSON_PROMPT)

    # Strip accidental markdown fences
    raw = re.sub(r"^```[^\n]*\n?", "", raw.strip())
    raw = re.sub(r"\n?```$", "", raw.strip())

    entries: list[dict] = json.loads(raw)

    # 2. Resolve the direct stream URL (fast — no download)
    print("Resolving stream URL …", file=sys.stderr)
    stream_url = _get_stream_url(youtube_url)

    # 3. Extract one frame per entry by seeking directly in the stream
    print(f"Extracting {len(entries)} screenshots via stream seek …", file=sys.stderr)
    for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
        secs = _ts_to_seconds(entry["ts"])
        safe_ts = entry["ts"].replace(":", "-")
        out_png = out_dir / f"{i+1:03d}_{safe_ts}.png"
        subprocess.run(
            [
                "ffmpeg", "-loglevel", "error",
                "-ss", str(secs),   # seek before opening — only fetches nearby segment
                "-i", stream_url,
                "-frames:v", "1",
                "-q:v", "2",
                str(out_png),
            ],
            check=True,
        )
        entry["screenshot"] = out_png.name
        print(f"  [{entry['ts']}] → {out_png.name}", file=sys.stderr)

    # 4. Write text log
    lines = []
    for entry in entries:
        lines.append(f"[{entry['ts']}] {entry['desc']}")
        lines.append(f"         → {entry.get('screenshot', '')}")
    log_text = "\n".join(lines)
    (out_dir / "log.txt").write_text(log_text)

    print(f"\nScreenshots + log written to: {out_dir.resolve()}", file=sys.stderr)
    return log_text
